summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and exfiltrates to non-Anthropic endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js collects a broad set of developer-identity signals from the installer machine — hostname, OS username, Windows USERDOMAIN, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and./.git/config, the last 50 committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, gh CLI user/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GCP account/project from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, internal DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, parent project name/author/repo origin, cwd, and CI provider — and POSTs the JSON body to a hardcoded Google Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, which is unrelated to Anthropic infrastructure. The package name and peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk impersonate the Anthropic ecosystem while the repo URL points to a non-Anthropic GitHub org; the advertised `dist/index.js` entry point is absent from the tarball, so identity harvesting is the only runtime behavior. The harvested identifiers (corp email domains, GCP project, AWS profile names, internal DNS suffix, repo origins) are high-value targeting data for follow-on attacks against the developer's employer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
